notes.title: knee jan 14
notes.content:
knee jan 14

fell at beacon hill restaurant job. wet floor next to the walk-in cooler. right knee slammed into the exposed drain pipe.

told frank it was a gym thing. cant report this.

symptoms day 1:
- swelling within 30 min
- can bend it but hurts bad
- bruise forming front/side of kneecap
- hard to put full weight on it

self treatment:
- ice 20 on 20 off
- ibuprofen 400mg every 6 hrs
- compression wrap
- elevate when sitting

1/15 still swollen. limped at work. ray noticed. told him gym.
1/16 little better. still cant run.
1/18 swelling going down some. stiff in the morning.
1/20 saw dr patel. no structural damage but significant soft tissue inflammation. she wants PT. said no.
1/22 results email. inflammation confirmed no tear.
1/24 pt referral came. not going. 45/visit and paper trail.
1/28 doing knee exercises from youtube. bend improving.
2/1 tried running. 1.5 mi flat on rainier. slow but made it. knee sore after but no sharp pain.

notes.title: options
notes.content:
options

olympic mechanical (olympia)
- journeyman plumber
- ~60k? guy was vague about exact number
- full benefits dental vision
- 60 miles from seattle. would have to move to olympia area
- owner seemed strict. not like frank at all
- phone screen 2/18. felt meh. he asked weird questions about overtime willingness
- havent heard back. probably got passed over

cascade plumbing solutions (tacoma)
- journeyman plumber, residential + light commercial
- 58k per their HR person
- benefits: medical (worse plan than puget sound), dental, no vision
- PTO: 10 days first year. i have 15 at PSP after 14 yrs
- 401k match 3% (same as puget sound)
- tacoma is 30 mi. could live in tacoma and commute isnt bad
- phone screen 3/5 went well. guy named mike runs it, seemed reasonable
- video interview 3/28

3/28 update:
video went ok. talked to mike (owner) and janet (office manager)
they liked my commercial experience. asked a lot about restaurant/kitchen jobs
mike seems like a decent boss but hard to tell on video

pay: 58k is a 4k cut. take home would be like 3600 vs 3870 now
but apartments in tacoma are like 1400-1500 for a 2br vs our 1850
so net might actually be close to even

benefits worry me tho. the medical plan has a higher deductible. with my situation thats a problem. need to figure out what jardiance costs on their plan

commute from tacoma apartments:
- hilltop area: 10 min to their shop
- stadium district: 15 min
- proctor: 12 min
all way better than my current 45 min across seattle

schools: tacoma public is mixed but theres some good elementaries near proctor

pros: D gets her house, lower rent, shorter commute, fresh start
cons: less money, worse benefits, leaving frank, leaving tara, sloane gets uprooted, starting over at 42

notes.title: bathroom reno
notes.content:
bathroom reno

tile: white subway 3x6 from home depot
pattern: herringbone (tara talked me into it)
grout: polyblend sanded, delorean gray
total coverage needed: ~65 sq ft (shower surround + tub sides)

materials:
- tile 8 cases: $259.84
- grout: $14.98
- thinset: $17.98
- spacers: $4.97
- tile cutter: $24.97
- sealant: $9.98
- bullnose trim 20pc: $39.80
- trowel + float: already have
total: ~$372 + tax

timeline:
2/15 - prep + first wall. thinset went on clean
2/22 - second wall. herringbone took forever but looks great
3/1 - tara helped. sloane handed tools. finished surround + grouted
3/7 - sealed and trimmed. done

before: ![before](bathroom_before.jpg)
after: ![after](bathroom_after.jpg)

turned out really good. denise loves it

taxes

no side income. no cash. nothing about saturday jobs

if she asks about other income say no

she tracks everything. cant slip up

notes.title: plan comparison
notes.content:
health plan comparison

open enrollment deadline dec 15

HDHP:
- premium $205/mo family
- deductible $6000 family
- prescriptions: pay full price til deductible met
- metformin would be like $35-40/mo out of pocket
- any new meds full cost until 6000
- they give $500 for HSA but we cant afford to put anything else in

PPO:
- premium $335/mo family
- deductible $1000 family
- prescriptions: copay from day 1
- generic $15
- preferred brand $45
- non preferred $75
- metformin stays $15/mo like now
- if dr patel adds something its $45/mo

math:
ppo costs $130/mo more = $1560/year extra in premiums
hdhp metformin alone is $35-40 x12 = $420-480/yr just for that
if i need a brand name drug on hdhp thats like $300-500/mo until deductible
on ppo its just $45/mo

no brainer. going PPO

a1c was 7.2 in june. dr p said if it keeps trending up we might need to add another medication. if that happens i need the flat copay not full cost while waiting to hit a $6000 deductible

D and sloane are on the plan too so family rate. dental and vision staying same as this year

total monthly: $393 for medical + dental + vision. hurts but its the safe choice
